repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Support Lemma 1 already packages exact-fiber finite metric selectors for OR/minimax native workflows

published_prior_art.cite: standard finite KKT/metric projection algebra; CarrascoFlorensRenault2007 inverse-problem regularization background

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Conjecture 1 already proposed a finite OR/DR-vs-minimax native-equivalence frontier with first-order equivalence language

published_prior_art.cite: none; KallusMaoUehara2021, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024, and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT do not give an OR/DR-vs-minimax row-commutator frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Conjecture 2/3 already target native selection-range separation and finite proximal witness geometry after ATE-active nonuniqueness

published_prior_art.cite: none; ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 do not characterize this two-selector open-class commutator separation

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024 supplies proximal OR/DR moments and efficiency theory, but the proposal treats it as publishing the residual selector Q^R(h)=E[(Y-h(W,x))^2|A,X]; that selector objective is not pinned to the cited equation/theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the closest penalized-minimax selector comparator BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023MinimaxIV, which proves convergence to a fixed solution without uniqueness/closedness, is omitted from the checklist and comparator chain.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed residual objective Q^R has a linear cross term b_w=E[Y 1{W=w}|A=a,X=x]; its exact-fiber minimizer is generally a shifted G-projection, not K_G(T,m), so the OR/DR selector formula is missing a load-bearing residual term.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Native-equivalence through first order is defined as equality of selected values and Gateaux derivatives, while C_0 is the selected-value discrepancy and C_1 is its tangent Jacobian; the iff frontier largely unfolds these definitions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Q^R with the actual published OR/DR population moment criterion, or state the proposal as a new ridge/GMM selector rather than attributing the selector to Cui et al.

Make the frontier non-tautological by characterizing C(P)=0 through an independent algebraic condition, such as row-space equality, metric-commutation, or a closed-form observable rank test not defined as the discrepancy itself.
Add Bennett et al. 2023 Minimax IV as a location-anchored comparator and state why its fixed-solution minimax result does not already settle the selector-frontier question.
Upgrade the witness to a fully specified finite observed law, including E[Y|W,A,Z,X], so the residual-selector and bridge-equation objects can be checked from one joint distribution.

recommended_next_step: Revise the selector definition and non-tautological frontier before another flagship-floor review; the current kernel is at best field-tier and has a load-bearing OR/DR objective error.
